description: This diagram illustrates the geometric relationships between two observation points, a central reference track, and associated sensor footprints within a conceptual cylindrical corridor. The two observation points are represented by blue squares, labeled "T" and "T+12 M Days", indicating a temporal separation between observations. A solid blue line represents the central reference track (e.g., a satellite ground track or orbit). A dashed orange line is depicted parallel to the blue line, positioned outside the cylindrical corridor. Four grey rectangular elements, two associated with each blue square, represent sensor footprints or observation swaths. Three distances are labeled: "s_h" indicates the horizontal separation between the "T" and "T+12 M Days" observation points; "d_h" indicates the perpendicular distance from the "T+12 M Days" point to the solid blue line (cross-track distance); and "l_c" indicates the perpendicular distance from the "T" point to the solid blue line (cross-track distance). The transparent cylindrical volume defines a spatial corridor around the central reference track.
